fact: Radar operators detected incoming planes approximately 50 miles out and were told to stand down because Army B-17s were expected from the mainland
source: At Dawn We Slept: The Untold Story of Pearl Harbor, Gordon W. Prange (1981); the Opana Point radar detection by Pvts. Lockard and Elliott is also documented by the National Park Service

fact: Eight battleships were moored at Pearl Harbor during the attack
source: Naval History and Heritage Command, U.S. Navy — 'Pearl Harbor Raid, 7 December 1941: Overview and Special Image Selection'

fact: Ammunition lockers were sealed under peacetime regulations, hampering the defensive response
source: At Dawn We Slept, Gordon W. Prange (1981); also discussed in the Roberts Commission Report (1942) on readiness failures

fact: The first wave of the Japanese attack consisted of 183 planes
source: The first attack wave comprised 183 aircraft. Widely documented; see Naval History and Heritage Command and 'Pearl Harbor' entry on Wikipedia citing Prange and Willmott

fact: 2,403 Americans were killed in the attack on Pearl Harbor
source: National Park Service, Pearl Harbor National Memorial — official casualty figures
